title: ActorContext
description:
Who is performing the action and under what authority.

Derived from the session JWT issued by `ssi_vault`.

auth_level.description: Current authentication level of the actor.

auth_method.description: The method used to authenticate this actor. Propagated from `IdentityContext.auth_method` during normalization. Used by the policy matcher for `auth_methods` rule filtering.

oauth_scopes.description: Scopes granted by the token (used for OAuth2 scope matching).

owner_did.description: DID of the entity that owns the tenant / agent box (from JWT `iss`).

requester_did.description: DID of the entity that initiated the action (from JWT `sub`). May be the same as `owner_did` for first-party requests.

user_did.description: Optional end-user DID when the action is on behalf of a customer.

AuthLevel.description:
Authentication strength of the current session.

Expanded to a 5-tier numeric hierarchy (1-5) to enable strict `>= min_auth_level` policy evaluations.

oneOf.description: Level 1: Static API Key (Long-lived, lowest assurance).
Level 2: Bearer Token (e.g., OAuth2 with scopes).
Level 3: Session Token (e.g., HMAC JWT, standard login).
Level 4: Verified Presentation (DID VP / EdDSA signed).

AuthMethod.description: The method used to authenticate a request. Included in audit events and policy evaluation.
